content:
Incident Report
Page 3 of 11
regarding a registered sex offender detail in Lewis County.
We then made teams of officers to contact both the shooting officers and the witness officers to take photographs and inventory their equipment. Due to the large number of people involved, my role was to answer questions from the officers and their representatives, make sure the process was being completed in a timely manner, and assist where needed.
At one point I contacted Detective Robb, who was photographing and collecting evidence from Pierce County Deputy Oleole. Detective Robb had conducted round counts on Deputy Oleole's equipment and I assisted in replacing the rounds back in the magazines before leaving to answer more questions from various representatives .
I contacted U.S. Marshal Ryan Kimmel, as he was one of the last subjects to be photographed and inventoried, and advised him of what the process would be. I was advised by Agent Kimmel and his supervisor they would need to make a phone call as they did not know if they would allow their equipment to be inventoried and rounds to be counted. I was later advised this would be allowed so I brought Agent Kimmel to Detective Brown, who was going to be conducting the photographs and inventory.
I assisted Detective Brown in unloading Agent Kimmel's magazines so Detective Brown could count and photograph the equipment. Agent Kimmel was asked if he would like to provide a statement and told me several times he was undecided. Later, Agent Kimmel told me he did not think he should provide a statement at that time because he was very tired.
After all officers were photographed, inventoried and/or interviewed, we were asked to respond to the scene at 7601 3rd Way Southeast, where we would later be asked to photograph and inventory the vehicles that were still on scene.
When I arrived I saw a large number of non-law enforcement personnel dressed in all black who seemed to be yelling derogatory statements towards the police. These subjects were located outside of the crime scene tape which had been placed around the area.
As I entered into the crime scene I could see the body of Mr. Reinoehl lying a short distance away from what I was told was the vehicle he had been in. I also saw several marked and unmarked police vehicles surrounding Mr. Reinoehl's vehicle. I saw numerous yellow placards marking fired casings, which were lying on the ground and on top of some of the vehicles. I observed what appeared to be bullet exit holes from one of the officer's vehicles which was facing the suspect's vehicle directly in front of it. I could also see what appeared to be bullet holes in the suspect's vehicle windshield.
During this time, Washington State Patrol was conducting scans and trying to document the crime scene. The crime scene was located near several large apartment complexes .
At approximately 0025 hours, Detective Brown, Detective Robb and I were assigned to photograph and inventory Deputy John Munson's patrol vehicle, which was sitting towards the edge of the crime scene. It was agreed that I would be a
rplwdir.x1
